translations.titulo: Diagnosis and Assessment of Alzheimer's Disease
translations.descricao: Alzheimer's disease is the most common form of dementia, characterized by progressive cognitive decline. Guidelines recommend structured assessment for suspected cognitive impairment in primary and specialized care. The assessment includes medical history, cognitive tests, and exclusion of reversible causes.
translations.sus.indicacao: Diagnostic assessment for patients with suspected cognitive decline or memory complaints, without routine population screening program.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Elderly over 65 years with risk factors such as family history, hypertension, or Down syndrome.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Diagnostic assessment recommended by the Alzheimer
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of dementia is approximately 55 million cases, with Alzheimer's representing 60-70%. In Brazil, it is estimated 1.5 million cases of dementia.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global annual incidence of dementia ~10 million new cases. For Alzheimer's, ~5-10 per 1,000 person-years in elderly >65 years.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Alzheimer's is the 7th leading cause of death globally, with ~2 million annual deaths. In Brazil, it contributes to 5-10% of deaths in the elderly.
